1	{
2	  "body": "Please provide as much information as you can: \n\n* **Suggested term label:**\n\nvesicle membrane tethering activity\n\n* **Definition (free text)**\n\nMF vesicle membrane tethering activity\nA membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges between the vesicle membrane and its target before they are in close apposition prior to vesicle docking and fusion.\n\n\n* **Reference, in format PMID:#######**\n\nPMID:19887069\n\n* **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term**\n\npombe\nuso1\ngrp1\nrud3\nsec3\nexo70\nsec15\ndrp1/sec39\n\n\n* **Parent term(s)**\n\nGO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity\nextend def to include \"vesicle\" \n\nThe binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex or organelle.\n\n\n\n\n* **Any other information**\n",

99	      "body": "Created new term **vesicle membrane tethering activity** (GO:7770062) as a child of [GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/QuickGO/term/GO:0140177).\n\n**Definition:** A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion.\n\n**References:**\n- `PMID:19887069` — Sztul & Lupashin 2009, *FEBS Lett*, \"Role of vesicle tethering factors in the ER-Golgi membrane traffic\" (as requested)\n- `PMID:19575650` — Yu & Hughson 2010, *Annu Rev Cell Dev Biol*, \"Tethering factors as organizers of intracellular vesicular traffic\" — a broad review covering tethering factors across intracellular trafficking pathways, including the plasma-membrane exocyst and CATCHR-family complexes (Munc13 has a CATCHR/MUN-domain fold and is a key synaptic vesicle tethering factor)\n\nA note on the second reference: you asked for an article that \"also covers synaptic vesicle transport and targeting.\" Yu & Hughson 2010 is a general review that includes synaptic-relevant tethers (exocyst, CATCHR/Munc13) within its broader scope. If you'd prefer a more synapse-focused companion reference, I'd suggest `PMID:23164531` (Hallermann & Silver 2013, *Trends Neurosci*, \"Sustaining rapid vesicular release at active zones: potential roles for vesicle tethering\") or `PMID:22794257` (Südhof 2012, *Neuron*, \"The presynaptic active zone\"). Happy to swap or add — just let me know.\n\nSynonyms added:\n- `vesicle membrane tether activity` (EXACT)\n- `vesicle tethering activity` (EXACT)\n\nPR: geneontology/go-ontology#31895\n\n---\n🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**\n- Model: `claude-opus-4-6`\n- Agent harness: claude-code\n- Triggered by: @raymond91125\n- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24372402903)",

Extended the definition of `GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity` to include \"vesicle\" alongside protein, protein complex, and organelle:\n\n> The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex, vesicle or organelle.\n\nThe change has been added to the existing open PR #31895 for this issue (same branch as the new child term `GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity`). SPARQL-QC passes.\n\n---\n🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**\n- Model: `claude-opus-4-6`\n- Agent harness: claude-code\n- Triggered by: @raymond91125\n- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24373255989)",

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0000972
name: transcription-dependent tethering of RNA polymerase II gene DNA at nuclear periphery
namespace: biological_process
def: "The chromosome organization process in which the DNA sequence containing a gene transcribed by RNA polymerase II is maintained in a specific location at the nuclear periphery. In S. cerevisiae, this process involves cis-acting DNA sequences such as the TATA box and upstream activating sequence (UAS) elements, trans-acting transcriptional activators, and also the 3'-UTR of the transcript." [GOC:krc, PMID:18614049]
is_a: GO:0051276 ! chromosome organization
created_by: krc
creation_date: 2009-09-10T12:25:11Z

[Term]
id: GO:0000973
name: post-transcriptional tethering of RNA polymerase II gene DNA at nuclear periphery
namespace: biological_process
def: "The chromosome organization process in which the DNA sequence containing a gene transcribed by RNA polymerase II is maintained in a specific location at the nuclear periphery even after transcription has been repressed." [GOC:krc, PMID:17373856, PMID:18614049]
synonym: "posttranscriptional tethering of RNA polymerase II gene DNA at nuclear periphery" EXACT []
is_a: GO:0000972 ! transcription-dependent tethering of RNA polymerase II gene DNA at nuclear periphery
created_by: krc
creation_date: 2009-09-10T12:28:04Z

id: GO:0010381
name: obsolete peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tethering
namespace: biological_process
def: "OBSOLETE. The attachment of a peroxisome to a chloroplast via molecular tethers that physically bridge their respective membranes and attach them to each other. The tethering may facilitate exchange of metabolites between the organelles." [PMID:17215364]
comment: This term was made obsolete because it represents a molecular function rather than a biological process.
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31877" xsd:anyURI

input.content:
## Summary

No ontology edits were made in this run. Both deliverables requested in issue #31863 are already merged at HEAD of `src/ontology/go-edit.obo`, matching the form approved by @raymond91125 in the final issue comment.

## Verification

### 1. New term `GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity`

Present in `src/ontology/go-edit.obo`:

```
[Term]
id: GO:7770062
name: vesicle membrane tethering activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion." [PMID:19575650, PMID:19887069]
synonym: "vesicle membrane tether activity" EXACT []
synonym: "vesicle tethering activity" EXACT []
is_a: GO:0140177 ! membrane-membrane adaptor activity
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31863" xsd:anyURI
created_by: dragon-ai-agent
creation_date: 2026-04-13T23:43:55Z
```

This is identical to the stanza approved in the final issue comment (2026-04-15).

### 2. Extended definition of `GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity`

Present in `src/ontology/go-edit.obo`:

```
def: "The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex, vesicle or organelle." [GOC:curators]
```

This matches the wording requested in the 2026-04-13 comment ("…protein, protein complex, vesicle or organelle").

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue analyzed; intent clear; both deliverables identified and verified
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: Confirmed both terms parse via `obo-grep.pl` and match the user-approved form (full `make travis_build` not run — ROBOT not installed in this eval environment, and no edits were made)
- [N/A] RESEARCH: No new references introduced; PMID:19887069 and PMID:19575650 were vetted in the original run
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: Both `GO:0140177` and `GO:7770062` located and inspected; related `*membrane tethering activity` terms (e.g. `GO:0180050`) reviewed for pattern consistency
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS: Existing term sits under the `membrane-membrane adaptor activity` branch, parallel to `protein-mitochondrial outer membrane tethering activity` (GO:0180050) under the sibling `protein-membrane adaptor activity` branch — consistent
- [N/A] EDITS: No edits required
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: Single `is_a: GO:0140177`; no over-asserted is_a; no over-specified logical definition (appropriate, since "vesicle membrane tethering activity" doesn't decompose into a clean genus–differentia pattern requiring an equivalence axiom)
- [N/A] SPECIALIZED-EDITS: Not a CHEBI/reaction/taxon-constraint/obsoletion case
- [x] METADATA: `created_by`, `creation_date`, `term_tracker_item`, `def` xrefs all present and correctly formed on `GO:7770062`; no metadata changes added to `GO:0140177` (existing legacy metadata correctly preserved)
- [N/A] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: ROBOT/`make travis_build` not available in this eval env, but no edits were made
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: PMIDs were validated in the prior run that introduced this term (PMID:19887069 Sztul & Lupashin 2009; PMID:19575650 Yu & Hughson 2010)
- [N/A] CHANGES-COMMITTED: Nothing to commit

## Rationale for no-op

The repository at HEAD already contains the merged resolution of issue #31863 (PR #31895 from the original `dragon-ai-agent` run). The state matches exactly the stanza @raymond91125 posted in the final issue comment, so no further ontology edits would have any effect.
